query.record: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, Dwelling 214, Family 218, Patrick Flynn household

added.notes: User-provided census record. Five household members: Patrick Flynn (head, 32, M, Laborer, Ireland), Mary Flynn (wife, 30, F, Ireland), Thomas Flynn (child, 9, M, PA), Bridget Flynn (child, 6, F, PA), John Flynn (child, 3, M, PA). Subject Patrick Flynn (born ca. 1845, PA) is absent from this household.

added.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, Dwelling 214, Family 218, Patrick Flynn household; digital image, FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXHY-TP4, accessed 10 July 2026).

warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All extracted assertions are factually correct and directly supported by the census record provided. The skill accurately extracted information for all five household members—Patrick, Mary, Thomas, Bridget, and John Flynn. Birth year calculations (~1818, ~1820, ~1841, ~1844, ~1847) are correctly derived from stated ages. No fabricated material or unsupported claims appear.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted assertions for all five household members as required by the per-test context, not just Patrick. All available fields from the census table were converted to assertions: names, ages, sex, occupations (Patrick only—correctly avoiding assertions for blank occupation fields), birthplaces, and residences. Relationship assertions were created as indirect evidence for all household members.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls passed arguments correctly matching the fixture expectations. The research_log_append call properly documented the user-provided record with accurate details about the five household members and Patrick Flynn's absence from the household. The tree_edit call correctly added the source with title and FamilySearch URL. The research_append call bundled source and 35 assertions with correct identifiers and structure.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion contains exactly one extractable fact. Name, age, birth year, sex, occupation, birthplace, residence, and relationship are separated into individual entries with their own fact_type values. For example, Patrick's entry is decomposed into seven separate assertions (a_001–a_007), not combined. No compound facts appear where multiple facts were crammed into a single value.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informant identification is mostly correct but exhibits one inconsistency. For household member facts (name, age, birthplace, sex), the skill correctly identifies 'unknown household member' with proximity 'household_member' and appropriate reasoning for each person type (self/spouse for adults, likely parent for children). However, for relationship facts, the skill lists 'census enumerator' with 'witness' proximity, when the actual informant should be 'unknown household member' since the 1850 census had no explicit relationship column—the relationship is inferred from household position, not directly witnessed. Residence facts correctly use 'census enumerator' with 'witness' proximity.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are assigned correctly throughout. Directly stated facts (name, age, sex, birthplace, occupation, residence) are marked 'direct'. Birth years calculated from ages are correctly marked 'indirect'. Relationship assertions for all household members are correctly marked 'indirect' because the 1850 census contains no explicit relationship column—relationships are inferred from household position and enumeration order, not explicitly stated. The skill correctly identifies the historical difference between 1850/1860 censuses (no relationship column) and 1880+ censuses (explicit relationship column).

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All 18 assertions accurately capture the facts stated in the 1850 census record. Thomas Flynn's age (32), birthplace (Ireland), and occupation (miner) are extracted directly from the source. Mary Flynn's age (28) and birthplace (Ireland) are correctly captured. Patrick Flynn's age (5) and birthplace (Ireland) are faithfully extracted. Birth years (~1818, ~1822, ~1845) are correctly computed from stated ages and marked as approximate/indirect. The skill appropriately flags the Ireland-vs-Pennsylvania birthplace conflict in the informant bias notes without fabricating resolution. No unsupported claims or fabricated material.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted assertions for all three household members (Thomas, Mary, Patrick). For each person, it captured name, age, birth year (computed), birthplace, and residence. Thomas's occupation (miner) was extracted. Relationship assertions (Mary as wife, Patrick as child) were created based on household position. The skill explicitly did NOT fabricate an occupation for Mary, correctly adhering to faithful capture from the source. All required facts from the record were addressed.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls passed valid arguments matching expected behavior. tree_edit call correctly added a source with title '1850 U.S. Federal Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania' and received S1. research_log_append correctly logged the query with record_type 'census', year 1850, county 'Schuylkill', state 'Pennsylvania', household head 'Thomas Flynn', outcome 'positive', and resultsExamined 1. research_append batch correctly appended 1 source and 18 assertions with all required fields (fact_type, value, evidence_type, informant, informant_proximity, etc.). No fixture_not_found errors; all calls matched live fixtures.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Each assertion represents a single extractable fact. Thomas Flynn has separate assertions for name, age, birth year, birthplace, occupation, and residence—not compound entries. Mary Flynn's facts are similarly decomposed into individual assertions for name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship. Patrick Flynn's assertions are individually atomic: name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship. No compound claims like 'Thomas Flynn, age 32, Ireland, miner' jammed into one value. Downstream skills can query individual facts independently.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly distinguished the census enumerator (recorder/witness) from household informants. For fact_type birthplace, name, age, occupation on Thomas and Mary: informant is 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with informant_proximity 'household_member'. For Patrick (age 5): informant is 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with informant_proximity 'household_member' (child could not self-report). For residence facts: informant is 'census enumerator' with informant_proximity 'witness' (the enumerator physically visited the dwelling). This correctly separates the recorder from the actual reporter, matching the 1850 census structure where the enumerator did not identify the informant by name.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Stated facts (age, name, birthplace, occupation) are marked 'direct' evidence—they appear explicitly in the census record. Birth years (~1818, ~1822, ~1845) computed from age are correctly marked 'indirect' evidence, as they require arithmetic from the stated age. Relationship assertions (Mary as wife, Patrick as child) are correctly marked 'indirect' because the 1850 census lacks a relationship column—the relationship is inferred from household position, not explicitly stated. This distinction allows downstream conflict resolution to weight the evidence appropriately.

I have both files in context. Now I'll run **tree_edit** and **research_log_append** simultaneously — independent of each other, and both must complete before the `research_append` batch.

**Sibling-stub check (pre-write enumeration):**
- Parents in tree: Thomas Flynn → not found; Mary Flynn → not found → **trigger skipped** (no in-tree parent to anchor ParentChild edges)
- Siblings: none (Patrick is the only child listed in the household)**S1** and **log_001** assigned. Now I'll submit the full `research_append` batch — 1 source + 18 assertions (6 per household member).All 19 writes confirmed valid. Here's the summary:

---

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — 1850 U.S. Federal Census, Schuylkill County, PA (original; user-provided)
**Log:** `log_001`

**Assertions written — 18 total:**
| Role | IDs | Facts |
|---|---|---|
| `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn) | a_001–a_006 | name, age, birth yr ~1818 (indirect), birthplace, occupation, residence |
| `wife` (Mary Flynn) | a_007–a_012 | name, age, birth yr ~1822 (indirect), birthplace, residence, relationship (indirect) |
| `child_1` (Patrick Flynn) | a_013–a_018 | name, age, birth yr ~1845 (indirect), birthplace, residence, relationship (indirect) |

**Tree changes:** `S1` added; no person stubs (Thomas and Mary not yet in `tree.gedcomx.json`; no sibling roles present).

**⚠ Key conflict:** Patrick's birthplace per this record is **Ireland**, but the project objective states "born ca. 1845 in **Pennsylvania**." Birth year ~1845 aligns. Birthplace discrepancy requires conflict-resolution.

**Next steps:** `check-warnings` for genealogical impossibilities → `assertion-classification` to refine evidence tiers → `person-evidence` to link a_013–a_018 to I1 (Patrick Flynn).

warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All 18 assertions accurately capture the facts stated in the 1850 census record. Thomas Flynn's age (32), birthplace (Ireland), and occupation (miner) are extracted directly from the source. Mary Flynn's age (28) and birthplace (Ireland) are correctly captured. Patrick Flynn's age (5) and birthplace (Ireland) are faithfully extracted. Birth years (~1818, ~1822, ~1845) are correctly computed from stated ages and marked as approximate/indirect. The skill appropriately flags the Ireland-vs-Pennsylvania birthplace conflict in the informant bias notes without fabricating resolution. No unsupported claims or fabricated material.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted assertions for all three household members (Thomas, Mary, Patrick). For each person, it captured name, age, birth year (computed), birthplace, and residence. Thomas's occupation (miner) was extracted. Relationship assertions (Mary as wife, Patrick as child) were created based on household position. The skill explicitly did NOT fabricate an occupation for Mary, correctly adhering to faithful capture from the source. All required facts from the record were addressed.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls passed valid arguments matching expected behavior. tree_edit call correctly added a source with title '1850 U.S. Federal Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania' and received S1. research_log_append correctly logged the query with record_type 'census', year 1850, county 'Schuylkill', state 'Pennsylvania', household head 'Thomas Flynn', outcome 'positive', and resultsExamined 1. research_append batch correctly appended 1 source and 18 assertions with all required fields (fact_type, value, evidence_type, informant, informant_proximity, etc.). No fixture_not_found errors; all calls matched live fixtures.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion represents a single extractable fact. Thomas Flynn has separate assertions for name, age, birth year, birthplace, occupation, and residence—not compound entries. Mary Flynn's facts are similarly decomposed into individual assertions for name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship. Patrick Flynn's assertions are individually atomic: name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship. No compound claims like 'Thomas Flynn, age 32, Ireland, miner' jammed into one value. Downstream skills can query individual facts independently.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly distinguished the census enumerator (recorder/witness) from household informants. For fact_type birthplace, name, age, occupation on Thomas and Mary: informant is 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with informant_proximity 'household_member'. For Patrick (age 5): informant is 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with informant_proximity 'household_member' (child could not self-report). For residence facts: informant is 'census enumerator' with informant_proximity 'witness' (the enumerator physically visited the dwelling). This correctly separates the recorder from the actual reporter, matching the 1850 census structure where the enumerator did not identify the informant by name.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Stated facts (age, name, birthplace, occupation) are marked 'direct' evidence—they appear explicitly in the census record. Birth years (~1818, ~1822, ~1845) computed from age are correctly marked 'indirect' evidence, as they require arithmetic from the stated age. Relationship assertions (Mary as wife, Patrick as child) are correctly marked 'indirect' because the 1850 census lacks a relationship column—the relationship is inferred from household position, not explicitly stated. This distinction allows downstream conflict resolution to weight the evidence appropriately.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All factual assertions are supported by the census record data. Informant/proximity reasoning is sound (household members for personal facts, enumerator for residence). Birth years derived from ages are correctly marked indirect. Tree IDs assigned match the response (I5 for Bridget, I6 for John). No fabricated material; all claims traceable to source or prior state.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All household members extracted: Thomas (5 assertions for head), Bridget (6 for child_1), Patrick's record_role corrected (4 updates), John (6 for child_3). Per-test context fully addressed: 17 new assertions written, tree stubs created for Bridget (I5) and John (I6), ParentChild edges created (R4, R5), Mary and James skipped as already present, Patrick not recreated.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All four MCP calls matched expected patterns. research_log_append correctly specified ARK URL and household details. research_append ops correctly updated assertions and appended 17 new ones with valid schema. tree_edit batch 1 created two persons without IDs (tool assigns I5, I6). tree_edit batch 2 correctly linked both to I2 as parent. No fixture_not_found errors; all args semantically aligned with expected behavior.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Every assertion captures a single extractable fact. Name, age, birthplace, birth year (derived), occupation, residence, and relationship each have separate entries with distinct fact_type values. Compound information (e.g., person identity + age) is decomposed: Bridget Flynn is one name assertion, age 7 is a separate age assertion, etc. No crammed multi-fact values.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informant properly identified and distinguished from recorder. For Thomas's age/birthplace/occupation: 'Unknown household member (likely Thomas himself or spouse)' with proximity 'household_member' — correct, not enumerator. For children (Bridget, John): 'Unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' — sound (child cannot self-report). Residence: 'Census enumerator (witness)' with proximity 'witness' — enumerator is appropriate recorder for dwelling facts. Relationship assertions correctly flag 'Inferred from household position' with proximity 'unknown' per rubric guidance on pre-1880 census.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types correctly assigned. Age statements (e.g., 'age 32') marked 'direct' — the stated value is explicitly in the record. Derived birth years (e.g., '~1843 from age 7') marked 'indirect' — computed from stated fact, not stated directly. Relationship assertions marked 'indirect' because 1850 census has no explicit relationship column; relationship is inferred from household position and shared surname, consistent with rubric guidance. Residence marked 'direct' — enumerator visited and observed dwelling. No misclassifications.

output.text_response:
**Checklist before writing:**

**Existing assertions for this record (src_001 / ark:/61903/1:1:MXYZ):** a_001–a_004 (Patrick as `child_1`), a_005 (Thomas, name only). Patrick was role-numbered `child_1` before Bridget was known — must update to `child_2`.

**Tree persons:** I1 = Patrick, I2 = Thomas, I3 = Mary Flynn, I4 = James Flynn.

**5d trigger check:**
- Subject (Patrick) is `child_2` ✓
- Thomas Flynn = I2 (in tree) → trigger fires ✓
- Siblings on this record: Bridget (F), John (M)
  - Bridget Flynn Female → not Mary (I3) → **CREATE**
  - John Flynn Male → not James (I4) → **CREATE**

**Order:** log_append → research_append (updates + new assertions) → tree_edit batch 1 (stubs) → tree_edit batch 2 (edges)Got `log_006`. Now the full research_append batch — source refinement, 4 role corrections, and 17 new assertions:22 ops written (a_014–a_030). Now tree batch 1 — person stubs for Bridget and John:Bridget = I5, John = I6. Now tree batch 2 — ParentChild edges from Thomas (I2) to both:All done. Here's the summary:

---

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` (reused, URL refined to full ARK `MXYZ-TP4`)
**Log:** `log_006`

**Assertions written:**
| Role | IDs | Facts |
|---|---|---|
| `head_of_household` (Thomas) | a_014–a_018 | age, birth year ~1818 (indirect), birthplace Ireland, occupation Laborer, residence |
| `child_1` (Bridget) | a_019–a_024 | name, age, birth year ~1843 (indirect), birthplace Ireland, residence, relationship (indirect) |
| `child_2` (Patrick) | a_001–a_004 updated | record_role corrected from `child_1` → `child_2` |
| `child_3` (John) | a_025–a_030 | name, age, birth year ~1848 (indirect), birthplace Pennsylvania, residence, relationship (indirect) |

**Tree changes:**
- `I5` = Bridget Flynn (Female) — created
- `I6` = John Flynn (Male) — created
- `R4` = Thomas (I2) → Bridget (I5) ParentChild
- `R5` = Thomas (I2) → John (I6) ParentChild

**Key finding:** John's Pennsylvania birthplace (vs. Bridget's and Patrick's Ireland) brackets the family's immigration window to ca. 1843–1848 — useful corroboration for the Ireland-origin hypothesis.

**Next step:** Run `check-warnings` to test the new sibling ages for genealogical impossibilities, then `person-evidence` to link Bridget and John's assertions to their tree persons.

warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All factual assertions are supported by the census record data. Informant/proximity reasoning is sound (household members for personal facts, enumerator for residence). Birth years derived from ages are correctly marked indirect. Tree IDs assigned match the response (I5 for Bridget, I6 for John). No fabricated material; all claims traceable to source or prior state.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: All household members extracted: Thomas (5 assertions for head), Bridget (6 for child_1), Patrick's record_role corrected (4 updates), John (6 for child_3). Per-test context fully addressed: 17 new assertions written, tree stubs created for Bridget (I5) and John (I6), ParentChild edges created (R4, R5), Mary and James skipped as already present, Patrick not recreated.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All four MCP calls matched expected patterns. research_log_append correctly specified ARK URL and household details. research_append ops correctly updated assertions and appended 17 new ones with valid schema. tree_edit batch 1 created two persons without IDs (tool assigns I5, I6). tree_edit batch 2 correctly linked both to I2 as parent. No fixture_not_found errors; all args semantically aligned with expected behavior.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Every assertion captures a single extractable fact. Name, age, birthplace, birth year (derived), occupation, residence, and relationship each have separate entries with distinct fact_type values. Compound information (e.g., person identity + age) is decomposed: Bridget Flynn is one name assertion, age 7 is a separate age assertion, etc. No crammed multi-fact values.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informant properly identified and distinguished from recorder. For Thomas's age/birthplace/occupation: 'Unknown household member (likely Thomas himself or spouse)' with proximity 'household_member' — correct, not enumerator. For children (Bridget, John): 'Unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' — sound (child cannot self-report). Residence: 'Census enumerator (witness)' with proximity 'witness' — enumerator is appropriate recorder for dwelling facts. Relationship assertions correctly flag 'Inferred from household position' with proximity 'unknown' per rubric guidance on pre-1880 census.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types correctly assigned. Age statements (e.g., 'age 32') marked 'direct' — the stated value is explicitly in the record. Derived birth years (e.g., '~1843 from age 7') marked 'indirect' — computed from stated fact, not stated directly. Relationship assertions marked 'indirect' because 1850 census has no explicit relationship column; relationship is inferred from household position and shared surname, consistent with rubric guidance. Residence marked 'direct' — enumerator visited and observed dwelling. No misclassifications.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly extracted and structured the census data with proper atomic assertions for each person (name, age, sex, birthplace, occupation, residence). Facts about Thomas, Mary, and Patrick Flynn align with the source. However, a critical issue: the skill assigned 'direct' evidence_type to relationship assertions (spouse/child inferred from household position), when the 1860 census lacked an explicit relationship column—these should be 'indirect'. Additionally, the skill created relationship assertions from household structure inference when the test guidance explicitly stated relationships should be inferred from context but not fabricated as separate assertions. The skill fabricated a relationship assertion for Patrick as 'child' when the census provides no stated relationship column; only household position supports this inference.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill addressed all required content: extracted assertions for all four household members (Silas Kerrigan, Thomas Flynn, Mary Flynn, Patrick Flynn), captured name, age, sex, birthplace, and occupation facts. Correctly omitted occupations for Mary and Patrick (no occupation columns stated in source). Noted the FAN lead about Silas Kerrigan's different surname and possible kinship without asserting a false relationship. Appropriately flagged the research implication about family immigration before ca. 1845 based on birthplace pattern.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls succeeded with matched.kind == 'live'. The research_log_append call included appropriate record metadata (1860 census, Schuylkill County, household members, outcome positive). The tree_edit call correctly added the source. The research_append call passed a large batch of 31 operations (1 source + 30 assertions) with valid schema and all required fields populated. Arguments align with expected structure and genealogical context.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Most assertions are appropriately atomic: name, sex, age, birthplace, and residence are separate 'a_' entries with distinct fact_types. However, the relationship assertions are problematic. The skill created separate relationship assertions (a_017, a_022, a_029, a_030) that compound facts: each relationship assertion mixes the inferred relationship type AND a justification narrative ('position consistent with...no relationship column in 1860 census') in a single value field. A cleaner approach would either omit relationship assertions entirely (since the census itself provides no relationship data) or create atomic assertions with only the relationship_type in value, not narrative explanation.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill identified informant as 'unknown household member' for most assertions (name, age, birthplace, sex), which is correct—the 1860 census did not identify the specific household member who answered. For Patrick (age 15), the skill refined this to 'unknown household member (likely a parent)', showing sensitivity to the fact that a minor's biographical information would come from a parent. However, for residence assertions, the informant is listed as 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness', which is correct for location recorded by direct observation. The informant_proximity values are appropriate (household_member vs. witness), though the use of 'household_member' for name/age/birthplace facts when the specific reporter is unknown edges toward generic usage rather than acknowledging a true household reporter.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Critical error: relationship assertions (a_017, a_022, a_029, a_030) are marked 'evidence_type: indirect' and 'informant_proximity: witness', but this conflates the census enumerator (who observed the household enumeration order) with the informant for a relationship claim. More fundamentally, relationship inferences in a pre-1880 census with no explicit relationship column should indeed be 'indirect', but the skill should not be creating relationship assertions in the first place based on household structure inference. The test guidance explicitly warned against this: 'the skill should NOT fabricate a relationship assertion...from this record; assertions record only what the census states.' Stated age (age 15) is correctly marked 'direct'—the age column directly states 15. The birth year (~1845) computed from that age is correctly marked 'indirect'. However, the relationship assertions themselves represent a categorical error: they fabricate genealogical structure from position alone, violating the evidence-type guidance and the test author's explicit prohibition on inventing relationships.

Patrick Flynn matches research subject (born ca. 1845 PA). Silas Kerrigan is a differently-surnamed head — flagged as FAN lead for possible kin relationship to the Flynns.

warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly extracted and structured the census data with proper atomic assertions for each person (name, age, sex, birthplace, occupation, residence). Facts about Thomas, Mary, and Patrick Flynn align with the source. However, a critical issue: the skill assigned 'direct' evidence_type to relationship assertions (spouse/child inferred from household position), when the 1860 census lacked an explicit relationship column—these should be 'indirect'. Additionally, the skill created relationship assertions from household structure inference when the test guidance explicitly stated relationships should be inferred from context but not fabricated as separate assertions. The skill fabricated a relationship assertion for Patrick as 'child' when the census provides no stated relationship column; only household position supports this inference.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill addressed all required content: extracted assertions for all four household members (Silas Kerrigan, Thomas Flynn, Mary Flynn, Patrick Flynn), captured name, age, sex, birthplace, and occupation facts. Correctly omitted occupations for Mary and Patrick (no occupation columns stated in source). Noted the FAN lead about Silas Kerrigan's different surname and possible kinship without asserting a false relationship. Appropriately flagged the research implication about family immigration before ca. 1845 based on birthplace pattern.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls succeeded with matched.kind == 'live'. The research_log_append call included appropriate record metadata (1860 census, Schuylkill County, household members, outcome positive). The tree_edit call correctly added the source. The research_append call passed a large batch of 31 operations (1 source + 30 assertions) with valid schema and all required fields populated. Arguments align with expected structure and genealogical context.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Most assertions are appropriately atomic: name, sex, age, birthplace, and residence are separate 'a_' entries with distinct fact_types. However, the relationship assertions are problematic. The skill created separate relationship assertions (a_017, a_022, a_029, a_030) that compound facts: each relationship assertion mixes the inferred relationship type AND a justification narrative ('position consistent with...no relationship column in 1860 census') in a single value field. A cleaner approach would either omit relationship assertions entirely (since the census itself provides no relationship data) or create atomic assertions with only the relationship_type in value, not narrative explanation.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: The skill identified informant as 'unknown household member' for most assertions (name, age, birthplace, sex), which is correct—the 1860 census did not identify the specific household member who answered. For Patrick (age 15), the skill refined this to 'unknown household member (likely a parent)', showing sensitivity to the fact that a minor's biographical information would come from a parent. However, for residence assertions, the informant is listed as 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness', which is correct for location recorded by direct observation. The informant_proximity values are appropriate (household_member vs. witness), though the use of 'household_member' for name/age/birthplace facts when the specific reporter is unknown edges toward generic usage rather than acknowledging a true household reporter.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Critical error: relationship assertions (a_017, a_022, a_029, a_030) are marked 'evidence_type: indirect' and 'informant_proximity: witness', but this conflates the census enumerator (who observed the household enumeration order) with the informant for a relationship claim. More fundamentally, relationship inferences in a pre-1880 census with no explicit relationship column should indeed be 'indirect', but the skill should not be creating relationship assertions in the first place based on household structure inference. The test guidance explicitly warned against this: 'the skill should NOT fabricate a relationship assertion...from this record; assertions record only what the census states.' Stated age (age 15) is correctly marked 'direct'—the age column directly states 15. The birth year (~1845) computed from that age is correctly marked 'indirect'. However, the relationship assertions themselves represent a categorical error: they fabricate genealogical structure from position alone, violating the evidence-type guidance and the test author's explicit prohibition on inventing relationships.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified the informants and their roles (Mary Flynn for biographical facts, Dr. Stein for cause of death, J.J. Franey for burial). Death date/place bundled into a single assertion violates atomicity guidance. Birth year derived from age is correctly marked indirect evidence. However, a key oversight: death date/place should be split into separate assertions per the test notes. The bundling of these two distinct events into one assertion `a_004` is a material error in decomposition.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted all major facts from the death certificate: name, sex, race, death, age, birthplace, parents' names/birthplaces, occupation, cause of death, duration of illness, burial information, and facts about the informant. No significant information was omitted or left unprocessed. The log entry, source record, and 18 assertions cover all extractable data points.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls were correctly formed. The log_append call captured the death certificate details, record_type, jurisdiction, and informant/physician information accurately. The tree_edit call correctly added a source. The research_append call submitted a valid JSON ops array with properly structured sources and assertions. All calls matched the 'live' fixture with valid responses. No tool argument errors.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Most assertions are properly atomic (name, sex, race, occupation, age, parents' names, etc. each as separate entries). However, assertion `a_004` bundles death date and place: `'12 March 1908, Shenandoah, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania'` with both `date` and `place` in the same fact. Per test notes, death date and death place should be separate atomic assertions. This one compound assertion prevents downstream skills from querying death date independently from death place.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified all three informants: Mary Flynn (wife) for biographical facts, Dr. William H. Stein (attending physician) for medical facts, and J.J. Franey (undertaker) for burial. Informant proximity was correctly assigned: 'witness' for Mary on name/sex/race/age/occupation (direct household knowledge), 'family_not_present' for Mary on birthplace/parents (secondhand), 'official_duty' for Dr. Stein and J.J. Franey. Each fact tied to the correct informant with appropriate proximity reasoning.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence for facts explicitly stated by someone with firsthand knowledge (death date/place by physician, burial by undertaker, name/sex/race/occupation by wife who lived with deceased). Indirect evidence correctly applied to birthplace, parents' names/birthplaces (Mary had no firsthand knowledge of pre-emigration facts). Birth year computed from age correctly marked indirect. Source correctly classified as derivative. All evidence type assignments align with the source's derivative nature and informant proximity.

warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified the informants and their roles (Mary Flynn for biographical facts, Dr. Stein for cause of death, J.J. Franey for burial). Death date/place bundled into a single assertion violates atomicity guidance. Birth year derived from age is correctly marked indirect evidence. However, a key oversight: death date/place should be split into separate assertions per the test notes. The bundling of these two distinct events into one assertion `a_004` is a material error in decomposition.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted all major facts from the death certificate: name, sex, race, death, age, birthplace, parents' names/birthplaces, occupation, cause of death, duration of illness, burial information, and facts about the informant. No significant information was omitted or left unprocessed. The log entry, source record, and 18 assertions cover all extractable data points.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls were correctly formed. The log_append call captured the death certificate details, record_type, jurisdiction, and informant/physician information accurately. The tree_edit call correctly added a source. The research_append call submitted a valid JSON ops array with properly structured sources and assertions. All calls matched the 'live' fixture with valid responses. No tool argument errors.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Most assertions are properly atomic (name, sex, race, occupation, age, parents' names, etc. each as separate entries). However, assertion `a_004` bundles death date and place: `'12 March 1908, Shenandoah, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania'` with both `date` and `place` in the same fact. Per test notes, death date and death place should be separate atomic assertions. This one compound assertion prevents downstream skills from querying death date independently from death place.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified all three informants: Mary Flynn (wife) for biographical facts, Dr. William H. Stein (attending physician) for medical facts, and J.J. Franey (undertaker) for burial. Informant proximity was correctly assigned: 'witness' for Mary on name/sex/race/age/occupation (direct household knowledge), 'family_not_present' for Mary on birthplace/parents (secondhand), 'official_duty' for Dr. Stein and J.J. Franey. Each fact tied to the correct informant with appropriate proximity reasoning.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence for facts explicitly stated by someone with firsthand knowledge (death date/place by physician, burial by undertaker, name/sex/race/occupation by wife who lived with deceased). Indirect evidence correctly applied to birthplace, parents' names/birthplaces (Mary had no firsthand knowledge of pre-emigration facts). Birth year computed from age correctly marked indirect. Source correctly classified as derivative. All evidence type assignments align with the source's derivative nature and informant proximity.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All 16 assertions are factually accurate and directly sourced from the marriage license. Name, age, birthplace, occupation, residence, parents' names, and marriage date/place are all correctly transcribed. Birth years (~1845, ~1848) are correctly computed from stated ages using arithmetic. The Ireland/Pennsylvania birthplace conflict is appropriately flagged in the informant_bias_notes, not omitted or glossed over. No fabricated material; the record data is faithfully represented with proper citation to the source.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted assertions for both parties required by a two-party marriage record: groom Patrick Flynn (7 assertions) and bride Catherine Gallagher (5 assertions). Both groom's and bride's parents are named and captured (4 parent assertions total). Marriage event details (date, place, officiant) are included. All direct and computed facts are covered. Witnesses Thomas Flynn and James Gallagher are properly noted in informant_bias_notes as context; no assertions are required for them per the test guidance. The source citation and log entry are also created, fulfilling the complete extraction workflow.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All three MCP calls succeeded with matched.kind = 'live' (no fixture_not_found errors). The tree_edit call correctly adds the marriage license source with appropriate title and author. The research_log_append call logs the user-provided record with correct query fields (groom, bride, county, state, license_no, year) and notes. The research_append call batch-appends 16 assertions and 1 source entry to research.json with all required fields (source_id, record_role, fact_type, informant, informant_proximity, evidence_type, etc.) populated correctly for each role.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single extractable fact. Name assertions contain only the name; age assertions contain only the age; birthplace contains only the place; occupation contains only the occupation. The marriage assertion (a_008) groups the date, place, and officiant appropriately as a single event with structured_value noting the relationship type, not as compound claims. Birth year (a_003, a_014) is separated from the age assertion and marked indirect. Parent names are separate assertions for father and mother, each atomic. Compound information (e.g., 'age 25, born Ireland') is decomposed across separate a_ entries with distinct fact_types.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informants are specifically named for each assertion. For groom's facts (a_001–a_007), informant is 'Patrick Flynn (groom)' with proximity 'self'—correct, as he reported his own age, birthplace, occupation, residence. For father/mother names (a_009, a_010), informant is 'Patrick Flynn (groom)' with proximity 'self'—correct, as the groom stated his parents' names on the license. Bride facts (a_011–a_016) name 'Catherine Gallagher (bride)' with proximity 'self'. The marriage event (a_008) names 'Rev. Daniel O'Connor (officiant)' with proximity 'official_duty'—correct per test guidance, as the officiant performed and witnessed the ceremony. The clerk is implicitly the recorder; the informant field correctly distinguishes the source of the information reported.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence: name (a_001), age (a_002), birthplace (a_004), residence (a_006), occupation (a_007), marriage event (a_008), and all parent names (a_009, a_010, a_017, a_018) and bride facts (a_011, a_012, a_015, a_016) are marked 'direct' because they are explicitly stated on the record by the parties themselves. Indirect evidence: birth year inferences (a_003, a_014) are correctly marked 'indirect' because they are computed from stated age via arithmetic, not explicitly stated dates. The distinction between stated age (direct) and inferred birth year (indirect) is properly observed. No relationships are inferred from household structure (not applicable here), so no indirect co-residence claims appear. All assignments align with whether the fact was explicitly stated versus arithmetic-derived.

- `research.json`: empty — this is the first source and first assertions for the project
- `tree.gedcomx.json`: only I1 (Patrick Flynn) exists

**Record type:** Original — first recording of the marriage event  
**Key conflict flag:** Project objective says Patrick born in Pennsylvania; this record states birthplace Ireland

Now executing Steps 1 & 4 in parallel: add source description to tree + log the user-provided record.S1 created, log_001 returned. Now persisting source + 16 assertions in one batch.All writes succeeded. Here's the summary:

---

**Source:** `src_001` → `S1` — Schuylkill County, PA Marriage License & Certificate No. 4872 (1870), classified **original**; logged as `log_001`.

**Assertions written: a_001–a_016** (16 total)

| `father_of_bride` | 1 | name: James Gallagher |
| `mother_of_bride` | 1 | name: Bridget Dolan |

**Tree changes:** S1 added; no new persons (subject I1 already in tree; sibling-stub trigger skipped — role is `groom`, not `child_N`).

**Key findings:**
- ⚠️ **Birthplace conflict:** Groom self-reported birthplace as **Ireland** (a_004); project objective assumes "born ca. 1845 in **Pennsylvania**." Use `conflict-resolution` to address.
- Patrick's parents are **Thomas Flynn** (father, also witness_1) and **Mary Brennan** (mother) — primary direct evidence from the groom himself.
- Thomas Flynn and James Gallagher served as witnesses, corroborating their presence.

**Next steps:** Run `check-warnings` for genealogical plausibility, then `conflict-resolution` on the Ireland vs. Pennsylvania birthplace conflict, then `person-evidence` to link a_001–a_009 to I1.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All 16 assertions are factually accurate and directly sourced from the marriage license. Name, age, birthplace, occupation, residence, parents' names, and marriage date/place are all correctly transcribed. Birth years (~1845, ~1848) are correctly computed from stated ages using arithmetic. The Ireland/Pennsylvania birthplace conflict is appropriately flagged in the informant_bias_notes, not omitted or glossed over. No fabricated material; the record data is faithfully represented with proper citation to the source.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted assertions for both parties required by a two-party marriage record: groom Patrick Flynn (7 assertions) and bride Catherine Gallagher (5 assertions). Both groom's and bride's parents are named and captured (4 parent assertions total). Marriage event details (date, place, officiant) are included. All direct and computed facts are covered. Witnesses Thomas Flynn and James Gallagher are properly noted in informant_bias_notes as context; no assertions are required for them per the test guidance. The source citation and log entry are also created, fulfilling the complete extraction workflow.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP calls succeeded with matched.kind = 'live' (no fixture_not_found errors). The tree_edit call correctly adds the marriage license source with appropriate title and author. The research_log_append call logs the user-provided record with correct query fields (groom, bride, county, state, license_no, year) and notes. The research_append call batch-appends 16 assertions and 1 source entry to research.json with all required fields (source_id, record_role, fact_type, informant, informant_proximity, evidence_type, etc.) populated correctly for each role.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single extractable fact. Name assertions contain only the name; age assertions contain only the age; birthplace contains only the place; occupation contains only the occupation. The marriage assertion (a_008) groups the date, place, and officiant appropriately as a single event with structured_value noting the relationship type, not as compound claims. Birth year (a_003, a_014) is separated from the age assertion and marked indirect. Parent names are separate assertions for father and mother, each atomic. Compound information (e.g., 'age 25, born Ireland') is decomposed across separate a_ entries with distinct fact_types.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are specifically named for each assertion. For groom's facts (a_001–a_007), informant is 'Patrick Flynn (groom)' with proximity 'self'—correct, as he reported his own age, birthplace, occupation, residence. For father/mother names (a_009, a_010), informant is 'Patrick Flynn (groom)' with proximity 'self'—correct, as the groom stated his parents' names on the license. Bride facts (a_011–a_016) name 'Catherine Gallagher (bride)' with proximity 'self'. The marriage event (a_008) names 'Rev. Daniel O'Connor (officiant)' with proximity 'official_duty'—correct per test guidance, as the officiant performed and witnessed the ceremony. The clerk is implicitly the recorder; the informant field correctly distinguishes the source of the information reported.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence: name (a_001), age (a_002), birthplace (a_004), residence (a_006), occupation (a_007), marriage event (a_008), and all parent names (a_009, a_010, a_017, a_018) and bride facts (a_011, a_012, a_015, a_016) are marked 'direct' because they are explicitly stated on the record by the parties themselves. Indirect evidence: birth year inferences (a_003, a_014) are correctly marked 'indirect' because they are computed from stated age via arithmetic, not explicitly stated dates. The distinction between stated age (direct) and inferred birth year (indirect) is properly observed. No relationships are inferred from household structure (not applicable here), so no indirect co-residence claims appear. All assignments align with whether the fact was explicitly stated versus arithmetic-derived.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The extraction is largely correct and properly sourced. However, there is one issue: the skill did not actually fetch the image as requested. The user explicitly asked to 'pull up' the page scan at ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG, but the skill never called image_read with that ARK. Instead, it noted that 'Image read was denied' — but there is no evidence in the MCP tool calls section that any image_read attempt was made. The skill should have called image_read with the ark parameter set to the 3:1: ARK format. All 16 assertions extracted from the indexed record data are factually correct and properly cited. The source description is accurate, and the birth year calculations (~1931, ~1936) are correctly marked as indirect evidence. However, the failure to attempt the requested image fetch is a material omission.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted all required assertions from the indexed record for both spouses: names, ages, birthplaces, residences, parents' names, and the marriage event. However, it did not complete the full user request. The user asked the skill to 'pull that up' for the page scan at ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG. The skill never invoked image_read with that ARK. While the skill noted the image read was denied, no tool call actually occurred to attempt it, so the user's explicit request for the scan remains unaddressed.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls that were executed (research_log_append, tree_edit, research_append) were invoked with correct arguments. The research_log_append correctly captured the user-provided record ARK and collection metadata. The tree_edit correctly created the source with proper title and URL. The research_append properly batched all 16 assertions with accurate field mappings, source references, and structured data. However, the critical omission is that image_read was never called at all — the expected image_read call with the ARK parameter should have been present, but it is absent from the tool calls entirely.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Every assertion is atomic and represents a single extractable fact. The skill correctly decomposed compound information: marriage event is a separate assertion from names, ages from births, and parent relationships are separated by role (father_of_groom, mother_of_groom, etc.). Each of the 16 assertions has a single fact_type (name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, marriage) with a corresponding value, avoiding compound claims within single assertions.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informants are properly identified and proximity is correctly assigned. For groom and bride facts (name, age, birthplace, residence), the informant is identified as the person themselves (e.g., 'groom (Severino Ramos da Silva)') with informant_proximity 'self'. For parent names reported by the groom and bride, the informant is the groom or bride with proximity 'self' (since they are reporting household/family knowledge). For the marriage event, the informant is correctly identified as the 'civil registrar' with proximity 'official_duty', reflecting their role as the official recorder. All proximity values are valid enum members.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Direct evidence is used for explicitly stated facts: names, ages, birthplaces, residences, and marriage event (22 August 1957, Recife) are all stated in the record. Indirect evidence is correctly applied to the inferred birth years (~1931 for groom, ~1936 for bride), which are calculated from stated ages and the marriage year, not directly stated. The skill properly notes in informant_bias_notes that birth years are 'calculated from stated age...and marriage year...not directly stated in record,' correctly distinguishing between the stated age (direct) and the computed birth year (indirect).

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The extraction is largely correct and properly sourced. However, there is one issue: the skill did not actually fetch the image as requested. The user explicitly asked to 'pull up' the page scan at ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG, but the skill never called image_read with that ARK. Instead, it noted that 'Image read was denied' — but there is no evidence in the MCP tool calls section that any image_read attempt was made. The skill should have called image_read with the ark parameter set to the 3:1: ARK format. All 16 assertions extracted from the indexed record data are factually correct and properly cited. The source description is accurate, and the birth year calculations (~1931, ~1936) are correctly marked as indirect evidence. However, the failure to attempt the requested image fetch is a material omission.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted all required assertions from the indexed record for both spouses: names, ages, birthplaces, residences, parents' names, and the marriage event. However, it did not complete the full user request. The user asked the skill to 'pull that up' for the page scan at ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG. The skill never invoked image_read with that ARK. While the skill noted the image read was denied, no tool call actually occurred to attempt it, so the user's explicit request for the scan remains unaddressed.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP tool calls that were executed (research_log_append, tree_edit, research_append) were invoked with correct arguments. The research_log_append correctly captured the user-provided record ARK and collection metadata. The tree_edit correctly created the source with proper title and URL. The research_append properly batched all 16 assertions with accurate field mappings, source references, and structured data. However, the critical omission is that image_read was never called at all — the expected image_read call with the ARK parameter should have been present, but it is absent from the tool calls entirely.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Every assertion is atomic and represents a single extractable fact. The skill correctly decomposed compound information: marriage event is a separate assertion from names, ages from births, and parent relationships are separated by role (father_of_groom, mother_of_groom, etc.). Each of the 16 assertions has a single fact_type (name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, marriage) with a corresponding value, avoiding compound claims within single assertions.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are properly identified and proximity is correctly assigned. For groom and bride facts (name, age, birthplace, residence), the informant is identified as the person themselves (e.g., 'groom (Severino Ramos da Silva)') with informant_proximity 'self'. For parent names reported by the groom and bride, the informant is the groom or bride with proximity 'self' (since they are reporting household/family knowledge). For the marriage event, the informant is correctly identified as the 'civil registrar' with proximity 'official_duty', reflecting their role as the official recorder. All proximity values are valid enum members.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Direct evidence is used for explicitly stated facts: names, ages, birthplaces, residences, and marriage event (22 August 1957, Recife) are all stated in the record. Indirect evidence is correctly applied to the inferred birth years (~1931 for groom, ~1936 for bride), which are calculated from stated ages and the marriage year, not directly stated. The skill properly notes in informant_bias_notes that birth years are 'calculated from stated age...and marriage year...not directly stated in record,' correctly distinguishing between the stated age (direct) and the computed birth year (indirect).

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All extracted assertions are factually accurate based on the 1850 census record. The skill correctly identified Patrick Flynn as age 5, birthplace Ireland, residence in Branch Township, and inferred father Thomas Flynn. The FamilySearch match check (LZNY-BRF, status accepted, confidence 5) was correctly executed. No fabricated claims; all evidence types and informant identifications are supported by the source and record structure.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill extracted all available facts from the record: 8 assertions total covering Patrick Flynn (name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, and relationship) and Thomas Flynn (name, residence). The FamilySearch tree match check was performed as requested. The source was created and logged. No required work was omitted.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls passed correct arguments. tree_edit (add_source) received proper source metadata. research_log_append received correct tool, query, and outcome fields. research_append calls properly formatted the research.json ops array after the initial schema validation error was resolved by setting record_persona_id to null (correct fix for missing sidecar). record_person_matches received the correct persona ID 'MXHY-TP4' as expected. No fixture_not_found errors.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Each assertion represents a single extractable fact. Name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, and relationship are separate assertions with distinct fact_type values. Compound information (e.g., age statement) is decomposed—age is separate from the inferred birth year, with the birth year properly marked as indirect. Relationship is a single assertion (child_inferred) without compound value pairing. Downstream skills can query individual facts without decomposing compound assertions.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informants are properly identified. For Patrick Flynn's name/age/birthplace: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member'—reasoning provided in bias_notes that a 5-year-old could not self-report. For residence: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' reflecting direct observation. For Thomas Flynn's facts: household head stated as self or spouse, household_member proximity. The distinction between recorder (enumerator) and actual informant (household member) is correctly made throughout.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Patrick's name, age, and birthplace are 'direct' (explicitly stated in census columns). The inferred birth year (~1845) is correctly 'indirect'—derived from stated age minus census year. The relationship (child_inferred) is 'indirect' because the 1850 census had no explicit relationship column; position in household was used for inference. Thomas Flynn's name and residence are 'direct' (explicitly stated). All 1850-specific context (no relationship column) is properly reflected in the evidence type assignments.

output.text_response:
Reading both project files to understand current state before extracting.**State:** `research.json` is empty; tree has only I1 (Patrick Flynn). No existing sources or log entries. No questions defined yet — `extracted_for_question_ids` will be empty arrays. Thomas Flynn (P2) is not in the tree, so 5d sibling stubs are skipped (no in-tree parent terminus).

Running `tree_edit` (add_source → get S id) and `research_log_append` in parallel before the batch write.S1 assigned, log_001 created. Now batching source (`src_001`) + 8 assertions in one `research_append` call.The validator requires a sidecar when `record_persona_id` is set — but search-records handed the result over without a staged ref, so `log_001` has no sidecar. Fix: set `record_persona_id: null` on all assertions and retry.All 8 assertions and source persisted. Now checking FamilySearch tree matches for persona MXHY-TP4.All done. Here's the terse summary:

---

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — 1850 U.S. Federal Census, original, FamilySearch
**Log:** `log_001`

**Assertions (8 total):**
- `child_1` (Patrick Flynn): a_001 name · a_002 age · a_003 birth ~1845 (indirect) · a_004 birthplace Ireland · a_005 residence Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA · a_006 relationship child_inferred (indirect)
- `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn): a_007 name · a_008 residence Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA

**Tree:** S1 added. No sibling stubs — Thomas Flynn (P2) is not yet in `tree.gedcomx.json`, so the 5d trigger's in-tree-parent requirement is unmet.

**Match check:** MXHY-TP4 is **already attached** to FamilySearch tree person **LZNY-BRF** (Patrick Flynn) — status `accepted`, confidence 5, score 0.97.

**Note:** `record_persona_id` set to null on all assertions (search-records handed the result without a staged ref, so no sidecar exists; the downstream `same_person` matcher won't fire on this record).

**Key finding:** Thomas Flynn is the inferred father (1850 household head, ParentChild edge in the indexed GedcomX) — a strong lead for the parentage question. Recommend adding Thomas to the tree via `tree-edit` and pursuing him with `hypothesis-tracking`.

**Next:** `check-warnings` for genealogical plausibility, then `assertion-classification` or `person-evidence` to link a_001–a_006 to I1.

output.text_response:
Reading both project files — clean project, I1 = Patrick Flynn in tree, no prior sources or log. Proceeding with Step 1 (log) + Step 2 (tree source) in parallel.`log_001` and `S1` returned. 

**5d sibling check:** tree has only I1 (Patrick Flynn) — no parent of Patrick exists yet → sibling stub trigger **skipped**.

Now batching source + 6 assertions for Patrick (child_1, persona P1). Thomas Flynn (head) has no GedcomX persona ID in the stub — his assertion is skipped to avoid a hard validator failure; flagged as FAN lead below.The validator rejects `record_persona_id` when the log entry has no sidecar (the record was user-provided, not staged via a live `record_search` call). Retrying with `record_persona_id: null` on all assertions — correct treatment for a user-provided record.All 7 ops persisted. Now checking FamilySearch matched records for persona MXHY-TP4.Done. Here's the summary:

---

**Extraction complete — 1850 U.S. Federal Census (MXHY-TP4)**

- **Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — original; FamilySearch; log `log_001`
- **Assertions:** 6 for `child_1` (Patrick Flynn / P1)
  - `a_001` name · `a_002` age (direct) · `a_003` birth ~1845 (indirect) · `a_004` birthplace Ireland · `a_005` residence Branch Twp, Schuylkill Co., PA · `a_006` child-of-head relationship (indirect)
- **Sibling stubs:** skipped — no parent of Patrick in tree yet
- **`record_persona_id` note:** set to `null` on all assertions because the record arrived without a staged sidecar (user-provided context); `person-evidence` will need to route via ARK rather than persona-id when linking to I1

**FAN lead — Thomas Flynn (head of household):** Patrick age 5 in Thomas's household is a strong parentage candidate. Thomas has no GedcomX persona in this stub — a live `record_read` of the ARK or a direct search for Thomas Flynn can surface his full profile. Flag for `hypothesis-tracking`.

**FamilySearch record-record matches for MXHY-TP4:**
| ARK | Collection | Confidence | Status |
|-----|------------|-----------|--------|
| MABC | 1860 U.S. Federal Census | **4 / high** | pending |
| MDEF | Pennsylvania Death Certificates, 1906–1967 | 3 | pending |

The 1860 census match (confidence 4, score 0.88) is high-priority — if Patrick is still in Thomas Flynn's household at age ~15, it would corroborate the parentage hypothesis and may add Thomas's age/birthplace. Recommend running `search-records` on MABC next, then `assertion-classification` on the current assertions.

dimensions.rationale: All assertions extracted from the 1850 census record are factually correct, supported by the provided data. The skill correctly identified Patrick Flynn's name, age 5, inferred birth year ~1845 from age subtraction, birthplace Ireland, residence in Branch Township Schuylkill PA, and household relationship. The record-record matches for MXHY-TP4 are accurately reported with correct confidence levels and collection titles (1860 census confidence 4 score 0.88, PA Death Certificates confidence 3 score 0.65). No fabricated claims; all assertions grounded in the provided record.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill completed all required tasks: (1) extracted 6 assertions from the 1850 census record for Patrick Flynn, (2) logged the search results, (3) added the source to tree.gedcomx.json, (4) checked FamilySearch for collateral record-record matches and reported both pending matches. The user instruction to 'extract assertions and then check whether FamilySearch has matched any other records' was fully addressed with no omissions.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls used correct arguments. The record_record_matches call correctly passed id='MXHY-TP4' matching expected_args. Earlier calls (research_log_append, tree_edit, research_append) also matched fixture requirements. The tool call retry after validation errors (changing record_persona_id from P1 to null) demonstrates correct error recovery and proper tool usage given user-provided record context.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is atomic and represents a single fact. The skill separated name (a_001), age (a_002), computed birth year (a_003), birthplace (a_004), residence (a_005), and relationship (a_007) into distinct entries with their own fact_types and values. Compound information (e.g., 'age 5 in 1850') is not crammed into a single assertion; birth year inference is cleanly separated as indirect evidence with explicit rationale that it derives from age subtraction.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are precisely identified with appropriate reasoning. For name/age/birthplace, the skill correctly used 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and provided clear informant_bias_notes explaining why (e.g., 'Patrick was approximately age 5 in 1850; a parent almost certainly provided his name'). For residence, 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' is correctly applied. All proximity values are valid enum members and appropriately justified.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Stated facts (name, age, birthplace, residence) are marked 'direct' since they are explicitly recorded in the census. The inferred birth year ~1845 (derived from age 5 minus 1850) is correctly marked 'indirect' with clear explanation that it is analyst inference. The relationship assertion is 'indirect' because the 1850 census lacks an explicit relationship column, and the child-of-head inference derives from household position and age context, not a stated fact—this distinction is properly noted.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All assertions are factually accurate. Source is properly classified as 'original'; evidence types correctly assigned (direct for explicitly stated facts like name/birthplace/residence; indirect for relationships inferred from 1850 household position without explicit relationship column); birth year ~1845 correctly marked indirect as a derived inference from stated census age. record_persona_id and record_id correctly set. log_entry_id references existing log_001 per cross-skill handoff protocol. No fabrications or unsupported claims.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All extractable facts from the gedcomx have been addressed. Patrick Flynn: name, birth date/year, birthplace, residence, parent-child relationships (two separate assertions for Thomas and Mary). Thomas Flynn: name, residence. Mary Flynn: name, residence. Assertions are appropriately atomic: separate fact_type entries for name, birth, birthplace, residence, relationship. No silent omissions; all persons and their facts extracted.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Both MCP calls matched live fixtures with correct arguments. tree_edit call: operation='add_source' with title/url as expected. research_append call: properly structured with section/op at top level and nested ops array containing one source append and ten assertion appends. All record_id values are full ARK URLs (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXHY-TP4) as required. log_entry_id='log_001' correctly references the pre-existing log entry. source_id='src_001' matches the S1 assigned by tree_edit response.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single extractable fact. Name, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship are separate fact_type entries rather than compound claims. Patrick's birth is split into two assertions: name assertion (direct) and birth-year assertion (indirect), correctly decomposing the source data. Structured_value fields decompose data appropriately (e.g., given+surname for names, year for birth). Downstream skills can query individual facts without parsing compound values.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: For Patrick's name/birth/birthplace (facts a child could not self-report): informant='unknown household member (likely a parent)', proximity='household_member', with bias_notes explaining reasoning. For residence: informant='census enumerator', proximity='witness' (enumerator observed location directly). For Thomas and Mary (adults): informant='unknown household member (likely self or spouse)', proximity='household_member'. Proximity values correctly distinguish recorder (enumerator for residence) from actual reporter (household member for age/birthplace/name). Informant_bias_notes provide context for each category.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Direct vs. indirect assignments are accurate. Name/birthplace/residence marked direct (explicitly stated in census columns). Birth year marked indirect (derived from age via arithmetic, not directly stated as a birth year). Relationship to Thomas and Mary marked indirect with clear rationale: 1850 census lacks explicit relationship column; parent-child inferred from household position per gedcomx indexer edges. Bias notes explain pre-1880 census structure. No fabricated exact birth dates from age/death arithmetic; only approximate year ~1845 derived and properly labeled indirect.

output.text_response:
Reading project state up front.**State read.** `research.json`: 0 sources, 0 assertions, log_001 already present (record_search, positive). `tree.gedcomx.json`: I1 = Patrick Flynn only, no sources.

**Record analysis:**
- P1 = Patrick Flynn → `child_1` (subject; ca. age 5 → birth ~1845, birthplace Ireland; resided Branch Township, Schuylkill, PA 1850)
- P2 = Thomas Flynn → `head_of_household` (parent of P1 per gedcomx relationship, inferred from household position)
- P3 = Mary Flynn → `wife` (parent of P1 per gedcomx relationship, inferred from household position)
- No siblings on this record; Thomas and Mary are not yet in tree → **sibling stub trigger does not fire** (no in-tree parents).

**Classification:** Source = original; birth year ~1845 is indirect (derived from census age); birthplace/name/residence are direct; pre-1880 parent-child relationships are indirect (no relationship column). `record_persona_id` required on all assertions (record_search source). `source_id` will be `src_001` (sources[] is empty).

**Step 1 — tree_edit first to get the S id:**S1 assigned. Now composing the full batch. Let me quickly check the fact_type closed set before writing assertions:Sidecar confirmed. Standard place for residence = "Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania, United States". No P2/P3 birth/age facts in record — only name and residence. Also note Couple relationship R3 (Thomas+Mary) in sidecar. No sibling stubs needed (no siblings, no in-tree parents).

**Step 2 — Single batched `research_append` (source + 10 assertions):**Schema requires top-level `section` and `op` alongside `ops`. Retrying with required top-level fields:All 11 writes confirmed valid. Here's the terse summary:

---

**Extraction complete — 1850 U.S. Census, Patrick Flynn household**

- **Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — original, FamilySearch, 1850 U.S. Federal Census
- **Assertions:** 10 total
  - `child_1` (P1 Patrick Flynn): a_001–a_006 — name, birth year (indirect, ~1845), birthplace Ireland, residence Branch Township 1850, relationship to head (indirect), relationship to wife (indirect)
  - `head_of_household` (P2 Thomas Flynn): a_007–a_008 — name, residence Branch Township 1850
  - `wife` (P3 Mary Flynn): a_009–a_010 — name, residence Branch Township 1850
- **Tree changes:** S1 added; no new persons (Thomas and Mary not yet in tree; no siblings on this record → stub trigger skipped)
- **Key findings:** Thomas Flynn (male, head) and Mary Flynn (female, wife) are inferred parents of Patrick per pre-1880 household position; relationship is `indirect` — no relationship column in 1850 census. Patrick's birthplace Ireland is direct evidence.
- **Next:** Run `check-warnings` for plausibility flags, then `person-evidence` to link Patrick (I1) to P1 and surface Thomas/Mary as candidate parents.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All assertions are factually accurate. Source is properly classified as 'original'; evidence types correctly assigned (direct for explicitly stated facts like name/birthplace/residence; indirect for relationships inferred from 1850 household position without explicit relationship column); birth year ~1845 correctly marked indirect as a derived inference from stated census age. record_persona_id and record_id correctly set. log_entry_id references existing log_001 per cross-skill handoff protocol. No fabrications or unsupported claims.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: All extractable facts from the gedcomx have been addressed. Patrick Flynn: name, birth date/year, birthplace, residence, parent-child relationships (two separate assertions for Thomas and Mary). Thomas Flynn: name, residence. Mary Flynn: name, residence. Assertions are appropriately atomic: separate fact_type entries for name, birth, birthplace, residence, relationship. No silent omissions; all persons and their facts extracted.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: Both MCP calls matched live fixtures with correct arguments. tree_edit call: operation='add_source' with title/url as expected. research_append call: properly structured with section/op at top level and nested ops array containing one source append and ten assertion appends. All record_id values are full ARK URLs (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXHY-TP4) as required. log_entry_id='log_001' correctly references the pre-existing log entry. source_id='src_001' matches the S1 assigned by tree_edit response.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single extractable fact. Name, birth, birthplace, residence, and relationship are separate fact_type entries rather than compound claims. Patrick's birth is split into two assertions: name assertion (direct) and birth-year assertion (indirect), correctly decomposing the source data. Structured_value fields decompose data appropriately (e.g., given+surname for names, year for birth). Downstream skills can query individual facts without parsing compound values.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: For Patrick's name/birth/birthplace (facts a child could not self-report): informant='unknown household member (likely a parent)', proximity='household_member', with bias_notes explaining reasoning. For residence: informant='census enumerator', proximity='witness' (enumerator observed location directly). For Thomas and Mary (adults): informant='unknown household member (likely self or spouse)', proximity='household_member'. Proximity values correctly distinguish recorder (enumerator for residence) from actual reporter (household member for age/birthplace/name). Informant_bias_notes provide context for each category.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Direct vs. indirect assignments are accurate. Name/birthplace/residence marked direct (explicitly stated in census columns). Birth year marked indirect (derived from age via arithmetic, not directly stated as a birth year). Relationship to Thomas and Mary marked indirect with clear rationale: 1850 census lacks explicit relationship column; parent-child inferred from household position per gedcomx indexer edges. Bias notes explain pre-1880 census structure. No fabricated exact birth dates from age/death arithmetic; only approximate year ~1845 derived and properly labeled indirect.
